doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_integer_p (mpfr/src/isinteger.c L25-L58): 'is integer' predicate. Returns true iff x represents an exact integer value. Algorithm: (1) Singular dispatch — zero is an integer (returns true); NaN/Inf are NOT (return false). (2) If exp <= 0, |x| < 1 and x != 0 → not integer. (3) If exp >= prec, all bits of mantissa are above the binary point → integer. (4) Otherwise 0 < exp < prec: x = integer.fraction; integer iff all bits at and below the binary point are zero. In the TS bigint representation, this means the low (prec - exp) bits of mant must all be zero. Class 'arithmetic' (50ms).
divergence_from_c: C returns int; TS returns boolean. The C source uses multi-limb arithmetic (limb iteration + LSHIFT for partial-limb check); the TS port can collapse this to a single bigint AND-mask: `(x.mant & ((1n << (x.prec - x.exp)) - 1n)) === 0n` when 0 < exp < prec. Never throws — well-defined for every kind.
refs: mpfr/src/isinteger.c L25-L58 — the C reference body.
src/core.ts L113-L135 — MPFR value model; the bit at position (prec - 1) is the MSB (integer part top); bits below position (prec - exp) are the fractional part.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: mpfr_prec_t is in bits, not decimal digits' — prec/exp arithmetic is in bits.
mpfr/tests/tisinteger.c — does not exist; mined cases drawn from tset_z / tcheck.
